quotes.text: complexity very, very bad. say again: complexity very bad. you not smart enough to manage it, nobody is.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug very impressed with type systems when young grug. type systems shiny and wonderful. then grug mass mass if/else on union type and mass mass casting everywhere and grug not mass impressed anymore.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug wonder why big brain take simple monolith, split into 200 microservices, then mass mass effort to make work like monolith again.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug have cardinal rule: never refactor and change functionality at same time. grug see mass mass senior devs do this and mass mass problems every time.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug say always, always, always implement thing completely wrong way first, then fix. abstraction is the enemy of understanding for grug.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug no mass mass like closure. closure make code look mass smart but grug mass mass debug closure and mass mass not fun.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: Premature optimization is the root of all evil.
quotes.author: Donald Knuth
quotes.source: Structured Programming with go to Statements, 1974

quotes.text: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place. Therefore, if you write the code as cleverly as possible, you are, by definition, not smart enough to debug it.
quotes.author: Brian Kernighan

quotes.text: There are two ways of constructing a software design: One way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies, and the other way is to make it so complicated that there are no obvious deficiencies. The first method is far more difficult.
quotes.author: C.A.R. Hoare
quotes.source: The Emperor's Old Clothes, 1980 Turing Award Lecture

quotes.text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
quotes.author: Linus Torvalds

quotes.text: I think that 'good taste' is much more about the ability to see and understand that simplicity — and to prefer it over cleverness.
quotes.author: Linus Torvalds

quotes.text: Bad programmers worry about the code. Good programmers worry about data structures and their relationships.
quotes.author: Linus Torvalds

quotes.text: C++ is a horrible language. It's made more horrible by the fact that a lot of substandard programmers use it, to the point where it's much much easier to generate total and utter crap with it.
quotes.author: Linus Torvalds

quotes.text: Adding manpower to a late software project makes it later.
quotes.author: Fred Brooks

quotes.text: There is no single development, in either technology or in management technique, that by itself promises even one order-of-magnitude improvement in productivity, in reliability, in simplicity.
quotes.author: Fred Brooks

quotes.text: Testing shows the presence, not the absence of bugs.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

quotes.text: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

quotes.text: The competent programmer is fully aware of the strictly limited size of his own skull; therefore he approaches the programming task in full humility.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra
quotes.source: The Humble Programmer, 1972 Turing Award Lecture

quotes.text: A little copying is better than a little dependency.
quotes.author: Rob Pike

quotes.text: Data dominates. If you've chosen the right data structures and organized things well, the algorithms will almost always be self-evident.
quotes.author: Rob Pike

quotes.text: This is the Unix philosophy: Write programs that do one thing and do it well. Write programs to work together. Write programs to handle text streams, because that is a universal interface.
quotes.author: Doug McIlroy

quotes.text: You can't trust code that you did not totally create yourself.
quotes.author: Ken Thompson
quotes.source: Reflections on Trusting Trust, 1984 Turing Award Lecture

quotes.text: Simple is not easy. Easy is near to our capabilities. Simple is about lack of interleaving. They are completely orthogonal.
quotes.author: Rich Hickey

quotes.text: Every time I see someone come out with an answer in a day, I know they don't understand the problem yet.
quotes.author: Rich Hickey

quotes.text: State. You're doing it wrong.
quotes.author: Rich Hickey
quotes.source: Are We There Yet?, JVM Language Summit 2009

quotes.text: Design is not making a UML diagram. Design is about taking things apart in such a way that they can be put back together.
quotes.author: Rich Hickey

quotes.text: I want the simplest possible thing that satisfies my actual requirements, and I want to understand every single line of it.
quotes.author: Jonathan Blow
quotes.source: Preventing the Collapse of Civilization, DevGAMM 2019

quotes.text: Software is in decline. We are in a situation where we have systems that work less and less well, and we are building higher and higher on top of them.
quotes.author: Jonathan Blow
quotes.source: Preventing the Collapse of Civilization, DevGAMM 2019

quotes.text: Each layer of abstraction is a layer of bugs, a layer of performance problems, and a layer of things you don't understand.
quotes.author: Jonathan Blow
quotes.source: Preventing the Collapse of Civilization, DevGAMM 2019

quotes.text: You have not experienced true fear until you have deployed a fix that works in staging but makes production worse.
quotes.author: Bryan Cantrill
quotes.source: Platform as a Reflection of Values, 2018

quotes.text: The hardest bugs are the ones where your mental model is wrong. You don't see the bug because you're looking at the world wrong.
quotes.author: Bryan Cantrill

quotes.text: 'Clean code' is just code that looks nice. But looking nice and being good are not the same thing at all.
quotes.author: Casey Muratori
quotes.source: Clean Code, Pair Programming, and Refactoring Debate, 2023

quotes.text: Weeks of coding can save you hours of planning.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation, naming things, and off-by-one errors.
quotes.author: Unknown (based on Phil Karlton)

quotes.text: It works on my machine. Then we'll ship your machine.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Some people, when confronted with a problem, think 'I know, I'll use regular expressions.' Now they have two problems.
quotes.author: Jamie Zawinski

quotes.text: There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.
quotes.author: Bjarne Stroustrup
quotes.source: The Design and Evolution of C++

quotes.text: It always takes longer than you expect, even when you take into account Hofstadter's Law.
quotes.author: Douglas Hofstadter

quotes.text: 99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs. Take one down, patch it around, 127 little bugs in the code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: 6 months of debugging can save you 5 minutes of reading the documentation.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A senior developer is just a junior developer who has Googled more things.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Code tells you how. Comments tell you why. Git blame tells you who to be angry at.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The code you wrote 6 months ago might as well have been written by someone else. And that someone was an idiot.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: 'Full-stack developer' is just a polite way of saying 'we couldn't afford to hire two people.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A good programmer is someone who always looks both ways before crossing a one-way street. Then copies the answer from Stack Overflow anyway.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The generation of random numbers is too important to be left to chance.
quotes.author: Robert R. Coveyou

quotes.text: The first rule of programming: if it works, don't touch it. The second rule: see the first rule.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Fixing one bug and introducing three more is called job security.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: If you're not embarrassed by the first version of your product, you've launched too late.
quotes.author: Reid Hoffman

quotes.text: Stop overthinking. Start shipping. The market will tell you what to build next.
quotes.author: Pieter Levels

quotes.text: Build 12 startups in 12 months. Most will fail. The ones that don't will change your life.
quotes.author: Pieter Levels

quotes.text: Charge more.
quotes.author: Patrick McKenzie (patio11)

quotes.text: Don't call yourself a programmer. Call yourself something that sounds like you make or save the company money.
quotes.author: Patrick McKenzie (patio11)

quotes.text: Do things that don't scale.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: A single meeting can blow a whole afternoon, by breaking it into two pieces each too small to do anything hard in.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: If you have ramen profitability, you're default alive. The most important thing in a startup is not to die.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: A startup is a company designed to grow fast. The only essential thing is growth. Everything else we associate with startups follows from growth.
quotes.author: Paul Graham

quotes.text: All non-trivial abstractions, to some degree, are leaky.
quotes.author: Joel Spolsky

quotes.text: The single worst strategic mistake that any software company can make: rewrite the code from scratch.
quotes.author: Joel Spolsky
quotes.source: Things You Should Never Do, Part I, 2000

quotes.text: Teach Yourself Programming in Ten Years.
quotes.author: Peter Norvig

quotes.text: Focused, hard work is the real key to success. Keep your eyes on the goal, and just keep taking the next step towards completing it.
quotes.author: John Carmack

quotes.text: If you want to set off and go develop some grand new thing, you don't need millions of dollars of capitalization. You need enough pizza and Diet Coke to stick in your refrigerator.
quotes.author: John Carmack

quotes.text: Implementation is the great filter. A lot of brilliant ideas die because nobody wants to do the unglamorous work.
quotes.author: John Carmack

quotes.text: The real lesson is: finish things. Not starting is fine. Not finishing is a problem.
quotes.author: Gwern Branwen

quotes.text: Given enough eyeballs, all bugs are shallow.
quotes.author: Eric S. Raymond

quotes.text: A language that doesn't affect the way you think about programming, is not worth knowing.
quotes.author: Alan Perlis

quotes.text: Optimization hinders evolution. Everything should be built top-down, except the first time.
quotes.author: Alan Perlis

quotes.text: The most important thing in software engineering is not which tools or methodologies you use. It's finishing the damn thing.
quotes.author: Steve Yegge

quotes.text: Any application that can be written in JavaScript, will eventually be written in JavaScript.
quotes.author: Jeff Atwood

quotes.text: The best code is no code at all. Every new line of code you willingly bring into the world is code that has to be debugged, code that has to be read and understood, code that has to be supported.
quotes.author: Jeff Atwood
quotes.source: The Best Code is No Code At All, 2007

quotes.text: More computing sins are committed in the name of efficiency (without necessarily achieving it) than for any other single reason — including blind stupidity.
quotes.author: W.A. Wulf

quotes.text: Legacy code is simply code without tests.
quotes.author: Michael Feathers

quotes.text: Duplication is far cheaper than the wrong abstraction.
quotes.author: Sandi Metz

quotes.text: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake. Also applies to production systems on a Friday.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Don't deploy on Friday. Actually, don't deploy on Thursday either because you might have to fix it on Friday.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text:
// TODO: fix this later
// Note: 'later' was 3 years ago
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary solution that works.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: My development process: 1) Google the error 2) Find a Stack Overflow answer from 2014 3) It works 4) I have no idea why
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Backend developers when they have to center a div: 'Maybe I chose the wrong career.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: My code doesn't have bugs. It just develops random features.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Documentation is a love letter that you write to your future self.
quotes.author: Damian Conway

quotes.text: Move fast and break things. Unless you're building medical software. Or financial software. Actually, maybe just move carefully.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A 10x developer is someone who makes 5 other developers 2x more productive. Not someone who writes 10x more code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Deleted code is debugged code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is delete everything and start with a clear head. But maybe git stash first.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: This meeting could have been an email. That email could have been a Slack message. That Slack message could have been nothing.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: We tried agile. We tried scrum. We tried kanban. We tried SAFe. Turns out the problem was never the methodology.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Docker: because 'it works on my machine' wasn't a good enough answer, so now we ship the machine.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: You're not Google. Your 3-person startup doesn't need microservices. It needs to ship.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Kubernetes: transforming the simple act of running a program into a 2000-line YAML existential crisis.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Impostor syndrome: where you're mass mass worried you're a fraud, surrounded by mass mass people who also think they're frauds.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: It's not DNS. There's no way it's DNS. It was DNS.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The three most dangerous things in programming: a developer with a soldering iron, a manager who codes on weekends, and a junior with git push --force.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: I spent 45 minutes naming a variable today. This is fine. This is normal. I am a professional.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Junior dev: 'I don't know what I'm doing.' Senior dev: 'I also don't know what I'm doing, but faster and with more confidence.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Everybody gangsta until someone runs SELECT * FROM users in production with no WHERE clause.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Client: 'Can you just add one small feature?' Narrator: It was not, in fact, a small feature.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The heaviest objects in the universe: neutron stars, black holes, and node_modules.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: When in doubt, use brute force.
quotes.author: Ken Thompson

quotes.text: The right thing is frequently a mass mass complex thing. Worse is better. New Jersey style wins.
quotes.author: Richard P. Gabriel

quotes.text: You aren't gonna need it.
quotes.author: Ron Jeffries / XP

quotes.text: Always leave the code better than you found it.
quotes.author: Robert C. Martin (Uncle Bob)
quotes.source: Clean Code (adapted from Boy Scout Rule)

quotes.text: Shipping beats perfection. Every time. No exceptions. Ship the ugly thing.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Your SaaS idea is probably a spreadsheet. Ship the spreadsheet first.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Build the audience before you build the product. Selling to strangers is hard mode.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Step 1: Get ramen profitable. Step 2: There is no step 2. Everything else is a luxury.
quotes.author: Unknown
quotes.source: Indie hacker culture (term from Paul Graham)

quotes.text: I don't compete on features. I compete on speed. Ship in days, not months.
quotes.author: Marc Lou

quotes.text: Revenue is vanity, profit is sanity, cash is reality.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The best marketing strategy for a developer is to actually ship something useful and let people find it.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The best funding round is revenue.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The three chief virtues of a programmer are: Laziness, Impatience, and Hubris.
quotes.author: Larry Wall

quotes.text: Any code of your own that you haven't looked at for six or more months might as well have been written by someone else.
quotes.author: Eagleson

quotes.text: All problems in computer science can be solved by another level of indirection... except for the problem of too many layers of indirection.
quotes.author: Butler Lampson (modified)
quotes.source: Attributed, with modification from David Wheeler's original

quotes.text: Debugging is like being the detective in a crime movie where you are also the murderer.
quotes.author: Filipe Fortes

quotes.text: Nothing is more devastating to a startup than building something nobody wants. And nothing is more common.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: I had a problem, so I decided to use threads. pNow roblems. I two have
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: In theory, there is no difference between theory and practice. In practice, there is.
quotes.author: Yogi Berra (applied to programming)

quotes.text:
Day 1: 'Implement user authentication system with JWT tokens'
Day 47: 'fix'
Day 48: 'fix fix'
Day 49: 'please work'
quotes.author: Unknown
quotes.source: Dev Twitter / every git log ever

quotes.text: AI will replace programmers the same way escalators replaced stairs. They didn't. There are just more floors now.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: YAML: where your entire infrastructure can be brought down by a single misplaced space.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: If you think you understand time zones, you don't understand time zones.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: One 11-line npm package going offline broke half the internet. We learned nothing and kept going.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The nice thing about standards is that there are so many to choose from.
quotes.author: Andrew S. Tanenbaum

quotes.text: No matter what problem you post on the internet, someone will suggest rewriting it in Rust.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: CSS is the only language where you can mass mass mass mass years of experience and still mass mass not center a div on the first try.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: To understand recursion, you must first understand recursion.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Machine learning is just spicy if/else statements.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The most effective debugging tool is still careful thought, coupled with judiciously placed print statements. Or yelling at a rubber duck.
quotes.author: Unknown (extended from Kernighan)

quotes.text: The difference between a junior and senior developer is that the senior developer writes FizzBuzz with 47 design patterns and a microservice architecture, then deletes it all and writes a for loop.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Genius is 1% inspiration, 99% finding a Stack Overflow answer that's only slightly wrong.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Everyone has a testing environment. Some people are lucky enough to have a totally separate environment to run production in.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Blockchain: a database, but slower, and everyone can see your data.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: At a startup, CTO stands for 'Currently The Only developer.'
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Sometimes the best debugging technique is to go to sleep and realize the answer at 3am while technically unconscious.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Users will always find a way to use your software in the worst possible way you never imagined.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: A bug becomes a feature the moment a customer depends on it.
quotes.author: Unknown
